verbatim_text: Okay so what I'm going to pose is that even in the current era of neural networks I think it's useful to think of language model failures in terms of these structured models. So failures of modern AI systems can be understood and fixed through the lens of structured world user and self models. Okay so this talk will be organized in three parts along each of the world user and self models. In the first part I'll start with my work on world models where I use intrep techniques to understand what's going on inside these neural networks and present evidence that they implicitly learn to build these kinds of structured models.
